entries.source: Failed: this host requires wss:// or Tailscale Serve. No TLS endpoint detected.
Failed: secure endpoint reached, but TLS fingerprint verification timed out. Check Tailscale Serve or gateway TLS and retry.
Failed: couldn't reach the secure gateway endpoint for this host.
Please enable ${labels.joinToString(", ")} in Android Settings to continue.
Always. Lurking in the shadows, exfoliating.
Canvas output appears here when OpenClaw opens an app surface.
Canvas output needs an active gateway connection.
Connect the gateway to load channels.
Telegram, WhatsApp, email, and other channels appear here after setup.
Some channel status checks did not complete.
Connect the Gateway to search sessions.
Setup code, endpoint, TLS, token, password, onboarding.
Run these on the gateway host:
For Tailscale or public hosts, use wss:// or Tailscale Serve. Private LAN ws:// remains supported.
Turn this on for Tailscale or public hosts. Private LAN ws:// remains supported.
Connect the gateway to load dreaming.
Entries appear after a dreaming cycle writes a narrative summary.
The diary is waiting for its first entry.
Setup code points to an insecure remote gateway. $remoteGatewaySecurityRule $remoteGatewaySecurityFix
QR code points to an insecure remote gateway. $remoteGatewaySecurityRule $remoteGatewaySecurityFix
Setup code has invalid gateway URL.
QR code did not contain a valid setup code.
Enter a valid manual endpoint to connect.
Connect the gateway to load recent logs.
Connect the gateway to load nodes and paired devices.
Linked phones and node hosts will appear here after pairing.
Device pairing admin needs elevated access. Connected nodes still work.

Your personal AI assistant.
Exfoliate! Exfoliate!

These permissions keep OpenClaw secure
and useful.
Choose what this phone can share with OpenClaw. You can change these later in Settings.
Setup code expired. Scan a fresh setup QR.
Gateway password is required. Enter it again or edit this connection.
Gateway password is invalid. Re-enter it or reset this gateway connection.
Gateway token is required. Enter it again or edit this connection.
Gateway requires this device identity. Re-authenticate or reset this gateway connection.
Saved authentication is invalid. Re-authenticate or reset this gateway connection.
Gateway authentication is not configured. Edit this connection and try again.
Gateway authentication needs review. Check gateway settings, then retry.

Review provider readiness
and configured models.
Connect your Gateway to view provider readiness.
Refresh to recheck provider readiness from your Gateway.
Start a new conversation and it will show up here.
Open Chat to start or resume the current session.
Connect the gateway to load usage.
Provider limits will appear here when your gateway reports them.
Android shows scheduled work status. Create and edit schedules from the desktop app.
Connect the gateway to load cron jobs.
Create recurring OpenClaw work from the desktop app.
Connect the gateway to load agents.
Connect the gateway to load approval requests in the app.
Exec approval requests will appear here while this phone is connected.
Chat tool calls waiting in the active session remain visible here.
Check that OpenClaw can speak clearly on this phone.
Showing ${visibleApps.size} of ${apps.size}. Refine search for more.
Selected or full photo access granted.
App list stays on this phone.
Clear this phone's saved gateway access and scan a fresh setup code.

Android can scan or paste an existing setup code, but this gateway does not expose setup-code generation to the app yet. Generate the QR/code on the gateway host with openclaw qr, then scan it here or paste the setup code below.
Required for `notifications.list`, `notifications.actions`, and forwarded notification events.
Send and search SMS from this device.
Only listed package IDs are forwarded.
All packages except listed IDs are forwarded.
Suppress forwarding during a local time window.
Blank keeps notification events on this device's default notification route. Set a key only to pin forwarding into a different session.
Connect the gateway to load skills.
Skills installed on the gateway will appear here.
Connect the gateway to load skill details.
Skill detail is not available in the current skills status.
Tip: stop listening to send the captured turn.
Your words and OpenClaw replies will appear here.
OpenClaw only listens when you start Talk or Dictation.
Mic sends turns; Talk keeps the conversation open.
Gateway is offline. Open Settings to reconnect.
Your gateway is active and ready.
Connect to your gateway to get started.
Use your Gateway QR or setup code

Verify the certificate fingerprint before trusting this gateway.

%1$s

The gateway certificate changed. Continue only if you expected this.

Old SHA-256:
%1$s

New SHA-256:
%2$s
Dreaming has not promoted durable memory entries yet.
No recent recall, daily, grounded, or phase signals were reported.
The short-term dreaming store is empty.
Refresh reads live state. Maintenance actions update the gateway diary/artifacts.
The diary is present, but it does not contain dated Dream Diary blocks.
The gateway did not find DREAMS.md or dreams.md in the active agent workspace.
\(diary.path) exists but has no readable content.
Connect a gateway to read dream diary entries.
The gateway did not return dream diary content.
Connect a gateway to load dreaming entries.
Connect a gateway to load dreaming phases.
The gateway did not return dreaming phase details.
Connect a gateway to inspect connected instances.
The gateway did not report any system presence entries.
Connect a gateway to load scheduled work.
The gateway has no visible cron jobs.
Skill status is available from the gateway.
Search ClawHub and install into this workspace.
Connect a gateway to load workspace skills.
Try a different search or refresh from the gateway.
The gateway returned totals without daily session cost rows.
The operator session will attach when the gateway is ready.
Start a chat and it will appear here.
Fetching recent activity from the gateway.
Connect to the gateway to load recent chat activity.
Start a chat and it will appear here.
Connect from Settings to load Skill Workshop proposals.
New proposals will appear here when agents draft skills.
Return to the queue and choose another proposal.
Select refresh to load the proposal body.
Matching proposals appear here after gateway refresh.
Opens card title and notes entry
Connect from Settings to load workboard cards.
Create a card or change the filter.
Connect from Settings to create, move, and dispatch cards.
Cards moved into this lane appear here.
Gateway setup, pairing, channels, and mobile node reference.
Mobile setup codes, QR, and node approval.
Fetching installed channels, accounts, and routing status from the gateway.
Checking installed channel clients and account state.
Install or enable channel plugins on the gateway, then refresh.
Gateway returned an unexpected channel status response.
Connect to the gateway to load installed channels, accounts, and routing status.
This disconnects, clears saved gateway credentials, and reopens onboarding.
OpenClaw can show approval prompts and event alerts when the app is not active.
Notifications have been denied. Enable them in iOS Settings.
Enable notifications to receive approval prompts and event alerts outside the app.
OpenClaw cannot determine the current notification permission state.
Choose system, light, or dark appearance
No gateway actions are waiting for review.
Enable Notifications to receive approval notifications while OpenClaw is not open.
Allow the gateway to request photos or video while OpenClaw is foregrounded.
Keep the screen awake while OpenClaw is open.
Run app, permission, and gateway-adjacent checks without editing setup.
Control what device context OpenClaw can expose to the gateway.
Disable to block camera capture requests from the gateway.
Allow active Talk sessions to continue while the app is backgrounded.
Controls whether location can be shared with gateway tools.
Used for new Chat and Talk sessions.
No gateways found yet. Use manual setup if Bonjour is blocked.
Refreshing connection, discovery, and device trust state.
Scan a setup QR code, paste a setup code, or choose a discovered gateway.
Tailscale is off on this device. Turn it on, then try again.
Discovered gateways and manual setup live here when the gateway has not connected yet.
Review this exec request before continuing. Your decision will be sent back to the gateway.
Enable “Discovery Debug Logs” to start collecting events.
No gateways found yet. Make sure your gateway is running and Bonjour discovery is enabled.

Exec approvals can only be reviewed while OpenClaw is open and connected.

Enable Notifications to receive approval notifications while OpenClaw is not open.
OpenClaw uses your calendars to show events and scheduling context when you enable calendar access.
OpenClaw uses your calendars to add events when you enable calendar access.

OpenClaw uses the camera when you scan a Gateway setup QR code or ask your paired Gateway or assistant to capture a photo or short video from this iPhone, for example to connect to your Gateway or show your assistant a document, device screen, or workspace.
OpenClaw uses your contacts so you can search and reference people while using the assistant.
OpenClaw discovers and connects to your OpenClaw gateway on the local network.
OpenClaw can share your location in the background when you enable Always.
OpenClaw uses your location when you allow location sharing.
OpenClaw uses the microphone for realtime chat, voice wake, and push-to-talk.
OpenClaw may use motion data to support device-aware interactions and automations.
OpenClaw needs photo library access when you choose existing photos to share with your assistant.
OpenClaw uses your reminders to list, add, and complete tasks when you enable reminders access.
OpenClaw uses on-device speech recognition for talk mode and voice wake.
Turn this device into a secure OpenClaw node for chat, voice, camera, and device tools.

The connected OpenClaw agent can use device capabilities you enable, such as camera, microphone, photos, contacts, calendar, and location. Continue only if you trust the gateway and agent you connect to.
Scan a QR code from your OpenClaw gateway or continue with manual setup.
Then scan the QR code here to connect this device.
Default host is localhost. Use your Mac LAN IP if simulator networking requires it.
Gateway rejected credentials. Scan a fresh QR code or update token/password.

Approve this device on the gateway.
1) `\(commandLine)`
2) `/pair approve` in your OpenClaw chat
\(requestLine)
OpenClaw will also retry automatically when you return to this app.
Use this if you received a setup code instead of a QR code.
Search and add contacts from the assistant.
OpenClaw reacts when any trigger appears in a transcription. Keep them short to avoid false positives.
Sending a new pairing request to your gateway...
Approve this request on your gateway. Talk will start automatically when approval lands.
This device needs gateway approval before Talk can use realtime voice. Audio will go directly from
No chats or approvals need you
Tap the message pill below to start from your watch.
Menu bar companion for notifications, screenshots, and privileged agent actions.
No quick settings for this channel.
Use Config for account, guild, action, and policy details.
OpenClaw could not load editable settings for this channel.
Select a channel to view status and settings.
Select a config section to view settings.
This tab is read-only in Nix mode. Edit config via Nix and rebuild.
Wildcard config entries are edited from their concrete key.
Optional override (phone number / chat id / Discord channel)
Do not fail the job if announce fails
Jobs are saved, but they will not run automatically until `cron.enabled` is set to `true` and the Gateway restarts.
Manage Gateway cron jobs and inspect run history.
Select a job to inspect details and run history.
Tip: use ‘New Job’ to add one, or enable cron in your gateway config.
This process looks expected for the current mode. Kill anyway?
When enabled, OpenClaw won't install or manage \(gatewayLaunchdLabel). It will only attach to an existing Gateway.
Tools for diagnosing local issues (Gateway, ports, logs, Canvas).
Deep links (openclaw://…) are always enabled; the key controls unattended runs.
Controls the macOS app log verbosity.
Writes a rotating, local-only log under ~/Library/Logs/OpenClaw/.
Check which process owns \(GatewayEnvironment.gatewayPort()) and suggest fixes.
Used for pnpm/node fallback and PATH population when launching the gateway.
Used by the CLI session loader; stored in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json.

Uses the Voice Wake path: forwards over SSH when configured,
otherwise runs locally via rpc.
Note: macOS may require restarting OpenClaw after enabling Accessibility or Screen Recording.
Tip: the session directory is returned by “Show panel”.
Unpause OpenClaw to run agent actions.
Click a discovered gateway to fill the SSH target.
Click a discovered gateway to fill the gateway URL.
Discover OpenClaw gateways on your LAN
Automatically start OpenClaw after you sign in.
Keep OpenClaw visible in the Dock. When off, windows still show the Dock icon while open.
Enable idle blinks and wiggles on the status icon.
Allow the agent to show and control the Canvas panel.
Allow the agent to capture a photo or short video via the built-in camera.
Allow signed tools (e.g. `peekaboo`) to drive UI automation via PeekabooBridge.
Show the Debug page with development utilities.
Quit only when you want to stop the menu bar app completely.
Processing messages through the local Gateway on this Mac.
Connected to a remote Gateway configuration.
Ready to run after you choose a Gateway connection.
Choose where the Gateway runs and how this Mac app reaches it.
OpenClaw starts and monitors the Gateway on this Mac.
Choose local or remote before the app can attach to a Gateway.
Pick whether this app owns a local Gateway or attaches to another host.
Local is best for this Mac. Remote is best when the Gateway already runs on a Mac Studio or server.
Use Tailscale Serve so the gateway has a valid HTTPS certificate.
Use Tailscale plus an SSH tunnel for stable private access.
SSH keeps the Gateway private; direct is best for HTTPS or Tailscale Serve.
User and host for the remote Gateway machine.
The WebSocket URL exposed by the remote Gateway.
Use wss:// for public hosts. ws:// is allowed for localhost, LAN, .local, and Tailnet hosts.
Used when the remote gateway requires token auth.
The current gateway.remote.token value is not plain text. OpenClaw for macOS cannot use it directly; enter a plaintext token here to replace it.
Gateway auto-starts in local mode via launchd (\(gatewayLaunchdLabel)).
Latest presence beacons from OpenClaw nodes. Updated periodically.
OpenClaw - Voice Wake live meter active
Starts a new session id for “\(key)”.
Keeps the last 400 lines; archives the old file.
Deletes the “\(key)” entry and archives its transcript.
OpenClaw is a powerful personal AI assistant that can connect to WhatsApp or Telegram.

The connected AI agent (e.g. Claude) can trigger powerful actions on your Mac, including running commands, reading/writing files, and capturing screenshots — depending on the permissions you grant.

Only enable OpenClaw if you understand the risks and trust the prompts and integrations you use.
OpenClaw uses a single Gateway that stays running. Pick this Mac, connect to a discovered gateway nearby, or configure later.
Retry remote discovery (Tailscale DNS-SD + Serve probe).
Tip: keep Tailscale enabled so your gateway stays reachable.
Tip: use Tailscale Serve so the gateway has a valid HTTPS cert.
Checks the real remote websocket and auth handshake.
Used when the remote gateway requires token auth.
The current gateway.remote.token value is not plain text. OpenClaw for macOS cannot use it directly; enter a plaintext token here to replace it.
These macOS permissions let OpenClaw automate apps and capture context on this Mac.
Required for local mode: installs `openclaw` so launchd can run the gateway.

Installs a user-space Node 22.19+ runtime and the CLI (no Homebrew).
Rerun anytime to reinstall or update.
OpenClaw runs the agent from a dedicated workspace so it can load `AGENTS.md` and write files there without mixing into your other projects.
Create the workspace on the remote host (SSH in first). The macOS app can’t write files on your gateway over SSH yet.
Tip: edit AGENTS.md in this folder to shape the assistant’s behavior. For backup, make the workspace a private git repo so your agent’s “memory” is versioned.
This is a dedicated onboarding chat. Your agent will introduce itself, learn who you are, and help you connect WhatsApp or Telegram if you want.
Pick Local or Remote in Settings → General whenever you’re ready.

On your gateway host: install/update the `openclaw` package and make sure credentials exist
(typically `~/.openclaw/credentials/oauth.json`). Then connect again if needed.
Click the OpenClaw menu bar icon for quick chat and status.
Open Settings → Channels to link channels and monitor status.
Enable Voice Wake in Settings for hands-free commands with a live transcript overlay.
Open the menu bar panel for quick chat; the agent can show previews
Enable optional skills (Peekaboo, oracle, camsnap, …) from Settings → Skills.
Couldn’t load skills from the Gateway.
Make sure the Gateway is running and connected, then hit Refresh (or open Settings → Skills).
Follow the guided setup from the Gateway. This keeps onboarding in sync with the CLI.
Wizard complete. Continue to the next step.
macOS access for notifications, capture, voice, and device context.
Use this if macOS prompts were skipped or permissions need a fresh walkthrough.
OpenClaw only asks for macOS capabilities when a feature needs them.
Allow agents to use device location when a tool asks for it.
Always may require System Settings to approve background location.
Recheck macOS after approving access in System Settings.
Control other apps (e.g. Terminal) for automation actions
Show desktop alerts for agent activity
Control UI elements when an action requires it
Capture the screen for context or screenshots
Allow Voice Wake and audio capture
Transcribe Voice Wake trigger phrases on-device
Capture photos and video from the camera
Share location when requested by the agent
This gateway requires an auth token
That token did not match the gateway
This gateway host needs token setup
This setup code is no longer valid
This gateway is using unsupported auth
Paste the token configured on the gateway host.
Check `gateway.auth.token` or `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN` on the gateway host and try again.
This gateway is set to token auth, but no `gateway.auth.token` is configured on the gateway host.
Scan or paste a fresh setup code from an already-paired OpenClaw client, then try again.
This onboarding flow does not support password auth yet.
Approve this device from an already-paired OpenClaw client.
This gateway requires an auth token from the gateway host.
Gateway token mismatch. Check gateway.auth.token or OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN on the gateway host.
This gateway has token auth enabled, but no gateway.auth.token is configured on the host.
Setup code expired or already used. Scan a fresh setup code, then try again.
This gateway uses password auth. Remote onboarding on macOS cannot collect gateway passwords yet.
Pairing required. In an already-paired OpenClaw client,
ssh tunnel did not open local port \(localPort)
OpenClaw needs notification permission to show alerts for agent actions.
OpenClaw captures the screen when the agent needs screenshots for context.
OpenClaw can capture photos or short video clips when requested by the agent.
OpenClaw can share your location when requested by the agent.
OpenClaw uses the local network to connect to your remote OpenClaw gateway over LAN, Tailnet, or SSH.
OpenClaw needs the mic for Voice Wake tests and agent audio capture.
OpenClaw uses speech recognition to detect your Voice Wake trigger phrase.
OpenClaw needs Automation (AppleScript) permission to drive Terminal and other apps for agent actions.
OpenClaw can access Reminders when requested by the agent for the apple-reminders skill.
Peek at the stored conversation buckets the CLI reuses for context and rate limits.
No sessions yet. They appear after the first inbound message or heartbeat.
Optional capabilities that become available when their requirements are met.
Enable ready skills, or install missing tools on the Gateway or this Mac.
Refresh after changing binaries, environment variables, or skill config.
Switches to Local mode to install on this Mac.
Control how agent shell commands are approved on this Mac.
Used when the companion UI cannot display an approval prompt.
Let bundled skill command-line tools run without prompting.
Bare names match PATH commands. Use a path glob for a specific binary.
Select an agent scope above to add trusted commands.
Commands that miss the allowlist follow the prompt and fallback policy above.
system.run requests are denied unless the policy changes.
Known commands can run; new commands use the prompt policy.
Agents can run shell commands on this Mac without allowlist checks.
Block agent shell commands on this Mac.
Allow trusted command patterns and handle misses with prompts.
Allow shell commands without checking the allowlist.
Ask only when a command is not trusted yet.
Public HTTPS via Tailscale Funnel (requires auth).
Local mode required. Update settings on the gateway host.
Start Tailscale to get your tailnet hostname.
Serve uses Tailscale identity headers; no password required.
Stored in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json. Prefer OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD for production.
Voice Wake requires macOS 26 or newer
The Voice Wake and push-to-talk controls are hidden on older macOS versions.
Wake phrases, push-to-talk, microphone input, and Talk Mode feedback.
Listen for a wake phrase before running voice commands. Recognition runs fully on-device.
Start a full voice conversation when a wake phrase is detected.
Start listening while you hold the key and show the preview overlay.
Push-to-Talk resumes when Talk Mode is turned off.
Play short sounds when Talk Mode switches between listening, thinking, and speaking.
Tap Right Option to interrupt speech and return to listening.
Short phrases that start voice wake detection.
Languages are tried in order. Models may need a first-use download on macOS 26.
OpenClaw reacts when any trigger appears in a transcription. Keep phrases short to avoid false positives.
Press start, say a trigger word, and wait for detection.
The new certificate is trusted by this device; this is commonly caused by certificate rotation.
This device could not verify the new certificate.
